title: LlamaCppRuntime per-model perf stats + observability events
goal: Wire per-model perf stats (tokens/sec, prompt_eval_ms, gen_eval_ms, VRAM_resident, time_since_last_call) and Flight Recorder FR-EVT-LLM-INFER-* event family for LlamaCppRuntime. Drives the §10.13 ModelRuntime Control Panel (AC-MODEL-RUNTIME-CONTROL-PANEL).
expected_diff_shape: New src/backend/handshake_core/src/model_runtime/llama_cpp/perf_stats.rs (Perf collector); FR event emit calls in generate.rs / score_embed.rs / lora_impl.rs; new FR event family registration.
unblock_conditions: MT-074 generate + MT-075 KV cache + MT-076 LoRA merged (events emit from those paths).
implementation_notes: Per AC-MODEL-RUNTIME-CONTROL-PANEL: §10.13 panel displays per loaded model: id + on-disk artifact path + SHA256, active adapter, KV-cache occupancy, LoRA stack, active steering vectors, ProcessOwnershipLedger row link, live perf stats (tokens/sec, VRAM resident, time-since-last-call). PerfStats struct: `{ total_prompts: u64, total_tokens_generated: u64, prompt_eval_ms_total: u64, gen_eval_ms_total: u64, tokens_per_sec_ema: f32 (exponential moving avg), vram_resident_bytes: u64 (from CUDA runtime if available, 0 on CPU), last_call_at_utc: DateTime<Utc> }`. Collected in generate path: timing markers at decode-prompt-start / first-token / last-token. EMA update on each call. VRAM polling via cuda-rs or llama_cpp_2 GPU stat API (fallback 0 when CPU). FR events: FR-EVT-LLM-INFER-START { model_id, request_id (v7), tokens_in_prompt }, FR-EVT-LLM-INFER-TOKEN { model_id, request_id, token_index, latency_ms } (sampled at every 16th token to avoid flood), FR-EVT-LLM-INFER-END { model_id, request_id, tokens_generated, total_ms, finish_reason }, FR-EVT-LLM-INFER-LORA-MOUNT { model_id, lora_id, strength }, FR-EVT-LLM-INFER-LORA-UNMOUNT { model_id, lora_id }, FR-EVT-LLM-INFER-KV-EVICT { model_id, evicted_entries, reason }, FR-EVT-LLM-INFER-CANCEL { model_id, request_id, at_token_index }, FR-EVT-LLM-INFER-CAPS-LOOKUP { model_id, caller }. Events serialized via existing flight_recorder appender. PerfStats exposed via IPC kernel.model_runtime.perf_stats(model_id) (Tauri command in MT-068 extension).
risk_if_missed: §10.13 ModelRuntime Control Panel has no data source; perf regressions invisible; cluster X panel work cannot land.
validator_focus: EMA computation correctness; sampled-event throttling on TOKEN (no flood); VRAM fallback on CPU = 0 (not undefined); per-request request_id uses v7.

builder_handoff_correction_20260525.operator_instruction: Operator clarified that completed/pass-like states were not validator-reviewed and instructed Kernel Builder to mark everything ready for validator handoff except the blocked item.
builder_handoff_correction_20260525.current_interpretation: Kernel Builder implementation handoff readiness only; no WP/Integration Validator pass or validator-reviewed completion is claimed.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: Kernel Builder believes implementation/supporting evidence is ready for Integration Validator handoff; validator review remains pending.

implementation_record.summary: Implemented per-model LlamaCppPerfStats with cumulative prompt/token/timing totals, tokens/sec EMA, last-call timestamp, time-since-last-call, and CPU/unsupported VRAM fallback of 0. Generation now resets and reads llama-cpp-2 safe timing APIs, records stats at terminal generation outcomes, and emits FR-EVT-LLM-INFER-START/TOKEN/END through the existing FlightRecorder sink with v7 request_id correlation and every-16th-token sampling. Raw prompt and token text are not persisted in the new telemetry payloads. FR event family constants and registry entries are present for START/TOKEN/END plus LORA/KV/CANCEL/CAPS IDs; only START/TOKEN/END generation emission is wired in this MT.

sources_checked: llama-cpp-2 0.1.146 local crate timing API: LlamaContext::reset_timings(), LlamaContext::timings(), LlamaTimings t_p_eval_ms/t_eval_ms/n_p_eval/n_eval
llama.cpp upstream perf context API surfaced by llama-cpp-2
local FlightRecorder LlmInference payload validator and FR event registry
selected_patterns: Use llama-cpp-2 safe timing methods instead of parsing printed perf logs.
Store per-model cumulative stats on LlamaModelHandle behind Arc<Mutex<_>>, mirroring the existing speculative stats sink pattern.
Reuse FlightRecorderEventType::LlmInference for additive phase-marked START/TOKEN/END payloads so existing validators and sinks continue to work.
Sample TOKEN telemetry every 16 generated tokens to avoid event floods.
rejected_options: Parsing llama.cpp stdout/stderr timing or memory breakdown logs because that is brittle and not a structured API.
Using nvidia-smi/NVML/CUDA-specific shellouts for VRAM because the current safe runtime path has no portable structured resident-VRAM API.
Adding raw prompt or generated token text to Flight Recorder payloads because the contract only requires IDs, counts, and timing telemetry.
Adding IPC/Tauri command wiring in this MT because the owned-file scope is handshake_core runtime/FR/test code and MT-068 extension owns IPC command surface.

red_test.note: Failed on missing events_llm_infer module, LlamaCppPerfStats API, FR registry variants, and LlamaCppRuntime::perf_stats before implementation.

residual_risks: No Integration Validator has reviewed MT-079; this record is Kernel Builder supporting evidence only.
Only generation calls update LlamaCppPerfStats in this MT. score/embed tests remain green, but score/embed do not yet add perf totals.

FR-EVT-LLM-INFER-LORA-MOUNT/UNMOUNT, KV-EVICT, CANCEL, and CAPS-LOOKUP IDs are registered but not all emission paths are wired; lora_impl currently lacks a FlightRecorder hook to reuse without broad plumbing.

The literal packet proof command without --test compiles unrelated integration tests and hit a rustc ICE; use the scoped proof command for MT-079 evidence until the broader unrelated compiler issue is resolved.
The native speculative suite has a residual env-gated failure with zero/missing draft rounds under the current GGUF/prompt; this may predate MT-079 but remains relevant to Cluster C validator review.
Cargo output still includes pre-existing crate warnings outside MT-079 files; no MT-079-specific warnings were observed in focused proof logs.

minimum_controls: TOKEN event sampling at every 16th token (verified by event count vs generated tokens).
EMA reasonable (test sequence of timing values produces expected EMA).
